title: Burjeel Holdings reports robust financial performance despite backdrop of regional unrest - Arabian
summary: Dr. Shamsheer Vayalil, CEO of Burjeel Holdings, said the healthcare provider continued to invest and expand despite a challenging regional backdrop.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Burjeel Holdings have posted their financial performance for the three-month period ended 31 March 2026, which saw revenue and patient visits swell despite the challenges that occurred as a result of the war between the United States and Iran.

There was an increase of 7.2 per cent year-on-year when it came to patient visits, which only served to underscore the robust demand for their services.

Operating cash flow increased to $43.8 million (AED161 million) – and revenue spiked to 5.1 per cent reaching $353.9 million (AED1.3 billion).

Burjeel Holdings have continued to invest and expand in the opening quarter of 2026, and that has reinforced their position as a community-based healthcare provider and high-acuity clinical services.

Another key indicator in their success has been a continued investment in clinical capabilities, following the launch of specialised centres oncology, cardiology, and rare diseases.

In addition to the above, Burjeel Holdings also entered into several strategic partnerships to bring international expertise to the UAE.

In his statement, Dr. Shamsheer Vayalil, Chairman & CEO of Burjeel Holdings, praised the UAE leadership for their guiding hands during the conflict.

“The first quarter of 2026 was marked by a challenging regional backdrop, and I would like to express our deep appreciation for the UAE’s leadership, whose decisive actions ensured stability, safeguarded communities, and enabled continuity across critical sectors. In this environment, our teams delivered uninterrupted care with the highest standards of safety and quality. The quarter once again demonstrated the resilience of our network and the strength of our operating model, translating into solid performance despite temporary disruptions,” said Vayalil.

He added that its financial success in Q1 was largely down to its unwavering commitment to invest, expand, and strengthen their capabilities across the UAE.

“Importantly, we did not pause our growth. We continued to invest, expand, and strengthen our capabilities, reflecting our long-term confidence in the UAE and the region. Demand for high-quality, specialised healthcare remains structurally strong, and we are well-positioned to capture this opportunity. We emerged from this period stronger, more resilient, and firmly committed to delivering sustainable growth and long-term value,” said Vayalil.

Some of the key highlights from the report include:

Excluding the impact of the Unified Procurement Program (UPP), normalized revenue growth was 9.7 per cent YoY.

Total patient volumes increased by 7.2 per cent YoY to 1.8 million in Q1’26, reflecting robust underlying demand and the strength of the Group’s network.

Inpatient volumes increased by 5.9 per cent YoY, reflecting resilient underlying demand.

EBITDA increased by 11.2 per cent YoY to AED201 million in Q1’26, with margins improving to 15.0 per cent from 14.2 per cent in Q1’25.

Net profit increased by 44.5 per cent YoY to AED57 million in Q1’26, with net margins improving to 4.2 per cent from 3.1 per cent in Q1’25.

The Group’s net debt / pre-IFRS 16 LTM EBITDA1 stood at 1.7x as of 31 March 2026, improving from 1.8x at the end of 2025, supported by strong EBITDA growth despite continued investment in network expansion across the UAE and KSA.

Operating cash flow increased by 13.1 per cent YoY to AED161 million, with solid cash conversion of 65 per cent supporting the Group’s strong balance sheet position.

Dividends: On 17 April 2026, the Annual Shareholder Meeting has approved a full-year dividend of AED120 million (c. AED0.02 per ordinary share) for 2025.
